---
title: "Index of Lecture Material"
subtitle: 161.251 Regression Modelling
author: "Presented by Jonathan Godfrey and Nick Knowlton"
date: "Semester 2, `r lubridate::year(lubridate::now())`"
output:
  html_document:
    theme: sandstone
    highlight_style: pygments
---
<!--- Data is on
https://r-resources.massey.ac.nz/data/161251/
--->

```{r setup, purl=FALSE, include=FALSE}
library(knitr)
opts_chunk$set(dev=c("png", "pdf"))
opts_chunk$set(fig.height=6, fig.width=7, fig.path="Figures/", fig.alt="unlabelled")
opts_chunk$set(comment="", fig.align="center", tidy=TRUE)
options(knitr.kable.NA = '')
library(tidyverse)
library(broom)
library(tsibble)
library(fable)
library(feasts)
library(lubridate)
```


<!--- Do not edit anything above this line. --->


##  Week 1 — Jonathan Godfrey 

[Lecture 1: Paper Overview](Lecture1.html) 
[Lecture 2: The Basics of Regression Modelling](Lecture2.html) 
[Lecture 3: Simple Linear Regression](Lecture3.html) 
[Lecture 4: Linear Regression Modelling with R](Lecture4.html) 
[Lecture 37: Appendix: Regression through the origin](Lecture5.html)

##  Week 2 — Jonathan Godfrey 

[Lecture 5: Checking the Assumptions](Lecture1.html) 
[Lecture 6: Outliers and Influential Points](Lecture2.html) 
[Lecture 7: What to do When Assumptions Fail](Lecture3.html) 
[Lecture 38: Appendix: Transformations in regression models](Lecture4.html)

##  Week 3 — Jonathan Godfrey 

[Lecture 8: Prediction in Simple Linear Regression](Lecture1.html) 
[Lecture 9: Introduction to Multiple Linear Regression](Lecture2.html) 
[Lecture 10: Testing in Multiple Regression Models (1)](Lecture3.html) 
[Lecture 39: Appendix: Models with Many Factors](Lecture4.html)

##  Week 4 — Jonathan Godfrey 

[Lecture 11: Testing in Multiple Regression Models (2)](Lecture1.html) 
[Lecture 12: Basic Matrix Algebra](Lecture2.html) 
[Lecture 13: Matrices and Linear Regression Models](Lecture3.html)

##  Week 5 — Jonathan Godfrey 

[Lecture 14: Comparison of Multiple Linear Regression Models](Lecture1.html) 
[Lecture 15: Models with binary predictors](Lecture2.html)

##  Week 6 — Jonathan Godfrey 

[Lecture 16: Polynomial Regression Models](Lecture1.html) 
[Lecture 17: Models using orthogonal polynomials](Lecture2.html) 
[Lecture 18: Piecewise Regression Models](Lecture3.html)

##  Week 7 — Jonathan Godfrey 

[Lecture 19: Linear Models with One Factor](Lecture1.html) 
[Lecture 20: Diagnostics and Multiple Comparisons for Oneway ANOVA](Lecture2.html) 
[Lecture 21: Factor or Numerical Covariate?](Lecture3.html)

##  Week 8 — Jonathan Godfrey 

[Lecture 22: Introduction to the Two Factor Model](Lecture1.html) 
[Lecture 23: Orthogonal Factorial Models](Lecture2.html) 
[Lecture 24: Factors with Interactions](Lecture3.html) 
[Lecture 40: Appendix: Nonlinear least squares](Lecture4.html)

##  Week 9 — Nick Knowlton 

[Lecture 25: The General Linear Model](Lecture1.html) 
[Lecture 26: Comparison of General Linear Models](Lecture2.html) 
[Lecture 27: Variable Selection](Lecture3.html)

##  Week 10 — Nick Knowlton 

[Lecture 28: Stepwise and Penalised Regression](Lecture1.html) 
[Lecture 29: Multicollinearity and Ridge Regression](Lecture2.html) 
[Lecture 30: Weighted regression](Lecture3.html)

##  Week 11 — Nick Knowlton 

[Lecture 31: Splines and LOESS: Flexible Regression Curves](Lecture1.html) 
[Lecture 32: Time-Indexed Regression: From Flexible Models to Trend](Lecture2.html) 
[Lecture 33: Regression Models for Trend and Seasonality](Lecture3.html)

##  Week 12 — Nick Knowlton 

[Lecture 34: Diagnosing Autocorrelated Errors](Lecture1.html) 
[Lecture 35: Regression with Autoregressive Errors](Lecture2.html)
